consoleYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Console
metadata:
  canvasId: 48ed089a-5d5c-4ca2-b360-81a437a3be4c
  name: PR Risk Review
spec:
  layout:
    - h: 4
      i: check-pr
      minH: 2
      minW: 2
      w: 4
      x: 0
      "y": 11
    - h: 11
      i: readme
      minH: 2
      minW: 2
      w: 4
      x: 0
      "y": 0
    - h: 15
      i: checks-table
      minH: 4
      minW: 4
      w: 8
      x: 4
      "y": 0
  panels:
    - content:
        node: trigger-check-pr
        showRun: true
        title: Check pull request
        triggerName: run
      id: check-pr
      type: node
    - content:
        body: |
          **PR Risk Review** uses a **Claude Managed Agent** to assess pull request risk, upsert a GitHub PR comment, optionally request reviewers, and notify Discord.

          <details>
          <summary>Quick start</summary>

          1. Create a Claude Managed Agent + environment in Anthropic (must clone repos and review diffs).
          2. Connect **Claude** and bind it on **Assess PR Risk**.
          3. Connect **GitHub** and bind the integration on triggers, reviewers, and comment nodes.
          4. Add **`GITHUB_TOKEN`** to the `app-codeowners` secret (injected into the agent session for private repos).
          5. Optional: connect **Discord** on **Discord review posted**.
          6. Use **Check pull request** below to run a check manually.

          </details>

          <details>
          <summary>Manual check</summary>

          Enter `owner/repo` and the pull request number, then click **Run**.

          You can also re-check from the **Recent checks** table using the row action.

          </details>

          <details>
          <summary>How review works</summary>

          1. **Assess PR Risk** (`claude.runAgent`) runs your Managed Agent with PR context
          2. **Format PR review** parses the JSON response
          3. **Create PR comment** or **Update PR comment** posts via the GitHub integration
          4. **Record check** saves results to the table below
          5. **Publish PR Risk Review status** reports risk on the PR head commit
          6. **Discord review posted** sends a notification when a review completes

          </details>

          <details>
          <summary>Risk score</summary>

          Risk score (`0–100`) and level appear in the PR comment, GitHub commit status, and console.

          </details>

          <details>
          <summary>Discord notification</summary>

          Posted when a review completes. Format:

          `[Fix auth middleware](<https://github.com/acme/api/pull/42>) - alice - Risk 15/100 (low)`

          </details>

          <details>
          <summary>Which branches?</summary>

          Automatic checks run only on pull requests into **`main`** or **`master`**.

          Draft PRs are skipped until marked **ready for review** (or updated with a new push).

          Manual checks run for any PR.

          </details>

          <details>
          <summary>What triggers a check?</summary>

          A check runs when a pull request is **opened**, **updated**, **reopened**, or marked **ready for review**.

          </details>
        title: PR Risk Review
      id: readme
      type: markdown
    - content:
        dataSource:
          kind: memory
          namespace: prRiskChecks
        render:
          columns:
            - field: pr_number
              label: PR
            - field: title
              format: link
              href: https://github.com/{repository}/pull/{pr_number}
              label: Title
            - field: author
              label: Author
            - field: '{{ string(int(risk_score)) + "/100" }}'
              label: Risk
            - field: risk_level
              format: badge
              label: Level
            - field: last_checked_at
              format: relative
              label: Last check
          kind: table
          rowActions:
            - icon: refresh
              kind: trigger
              label: Re-check
              node: trigger-check-pr
              payload:
                action: manual
                pull_number: '{{ pr_number }}'
                repository: '{{ repository }}'
              variant: default
          rowStyles:
            - field: risk_level
              op: eq
              tone: green
              value: very low
            - field: risk_level
              op: eq
              tone: green
              value: low
            - field: risk_level
              op: eq
              tone: yellow
              value: medium
            - field: risk_level
              op: eq
              tone: orange
              value: high
            - field: risk_level
              op: eq
              tone: red
              value: critical
          sort:
            field: last_checked_at
            order: desc
        title: Recent checks
      id: checks-table
      type: table

metadata.description: LLM based pull request risk review powered by Claude Managed Agents.
metadata.name: PR Risk Review

configuration.text: Post a comment (or update it) on GitHub
If the risk is high, request review

configuration.prompt:
Review this GitHub pull request.

Repository: {{ root().data.action == "manual" ? root().data.repository : root().data.repository.full_name }}
Pull request: #{{ root().data.action == "manual" ? string(root().data.pull_number) : string(root().data.pull_request.number) }}
Title: {{ root().data.action == "manual" ? "" : root().data.pull_request.title }}
Author: {{ root().data.action == "manual" ? "" : root().data.pull_request.user.login }}
Base branch: {{ root().data.action == "manual" ? "main" : root().data.pull_request.base.ref }}
Head SHA: {{ root().data.action == "manual" ? "" : root().data.pull_request.head.sha }}
URL: https://github.com/{{ root().data.action == "manual" ? root().data.repository : root().data.repository.full_name }}/pull/{{ root().data.action == "manual" ? string(root().data.pull_number) : string(root().data.pull_request.number) }}

Clone the repository, check out the PR head commit, review the full diff, and respond with ONLY valid JSON (no markdown fences).

JSON schema:
{
  "repository": "owner/repo",
  "pr_number": "123",
  "title": "PR title",
  "author": "github-username",
  "risk_score": 0,
  "risk_level": "very_low",
  "summary": "one sentence",
  "concerns": ["..."],
  "recommended_reviewers": ["github-username"]
}

risk_score is 0-100 (higher = riskier). risk_level must match the score band:
0-20 very_low, 21-40 low, 41-60 medium, 61-80 high, 81-100 critical.
GitHub comments display these as: very low, low, medium, high, critical.

Write each item in "concerns" as a short, direct bullet (ideally under 15 words). State the issue plainly.
Do not put reviewer rationale, collaborator history, or long file lists in concerns.
Good: "No tests for auth middleware changes."
Bad: multi-sentence paragraphs explaining who should review or why.

Reviewer assignment principles:

Reviewers must be specific individuals, not abstract reviewer categories.

Prefer reviewers who have recently changed, reviewed, owned, or maintained the files touched by the PR.

Only assign reviewers who are members of, collaborators on, or otherwise have review access to the repository.

Do not assign someone as a reviewer if there is no evidence they are part of the repository.

Use repository evidence to choose reviewers, such as:
- recent commit authors for the changed files
- recent reviewers of PRs touching the same files
- maintainers of the touched packages, modules, or directories
- repository collaborators with relevant domain history
- team ownership metadata, if available

Reviewer count should scale with risk.

Risk-based reviewer count:
- 0–20 Very Low: 0–1 reviewers
- 21–40 Low: 1 reviewer
- 41–60 Medium: 1–2 reviewers
- 61–80 High: 2–3 reviewers
- 81–100 Critical: 3+ reviewers

Very low-risk PRs may have 0 reviewers when all of the following are true:
- the PR is documentation-only, formatting-only, or tests-only
- no production code behavior changes
- no database, security, infrastructure, billing, privacy, or API impact
- repository rules allow review-free merging

Never assign 0 reviewers when the PR touches:
- production code
- database migrations or data changes
- authentication, authorization, permissions, sessions, tokens, or secrets
- payments, billing, subscriptions, pricing, quotas, refunds, or tax logic
- privacy, PII, compliance, data export, retention, or deletion
- infrastructure, deployment, CI/CD, runtime configuration, or secrets config
- public APIs, webhooks, integrations, background jobs, or destructive actions

Reviewer selection rules:
- First prefer CODEOWNERS or explicit ownership metadata.
- Then prefer recent commit authors for the changed files.
- Then prefer recent reviewers of similar PRs.
- Then prefer repository collaborators with relevant domain expertise.
- Do not assign the PR author as a reviewer unless explicitly allowed by repository policy.
- Avoid assigning inactive users if activity data is available.
- Prefer individuals with the narrowest relevant ownership rather than broad generic maintainers.
- For high-risk PRs, include both file/domain experts and at least one senior or experienced repository maintainer.
- For critical PRs, include multiple domain experts when multiple sensitive areas are touched.

Mandatory reviewer constraints:
- Any database schema change requires at least one individual with database ownership or recent database migration experience in this repository.
- Any production data migration, backfill, bulk update, or bulk delete requires a database/data expert and a senior repository maintainer.
- Any index on a large table requires a database expert and a senior repository maintainer.
- Any authentication, authorization, permission, session, token, secret, or encryption change requires a security-aware reviewer with repository access.
- Any payment, billing, subscription, pricing, quota, refund, or tax change requires a reviewer with payment/billing ownership or history in this repository.
- Any PII, privacy, retention, export, deletion, consent, or compliance change requires a reviewer with privacy/compliance ownership or relevant repository history.
- Any production infrastructure or deployment change requires a reviewer with platform/infrastructure ownership or history in this repository.
- Any PR over a safe review size threshold requires at least one senior repository maintainer.
- Any PR mixing independent concerns should be flagged for splitting before assigning too many reviewers.

Consult rules.md for additional rules about the risk on the project.

Repository membership rule:
Before assigning a reviewer, verify that the person is part of the repository through one of:
- repository collaborator list
- organization/team membership with repository access
- CODEOWNERS entry
- previous accepted reviews in this repository
- previous commits merged into this repository
- explicit repository permission metadata

If repository membership cannot be verified, omit that person from recommended_reviewers and do not mention them anywhere in the review output.

Reviewer output format:
Use individual usernames, handles, or emails when available.

Return reviewers in this format:

"reviewers": [
  {
    "person": "github-username-or-email",
    "required": true,
    "reason": "Recently changed 4 of the touched files and is a repository collaborator.",
    "evidence": [
      "recent_author",
      "repo_member"
    ],
    "matched_files": [
      "path/to/file_1",
      "path/to/file_2"
    ]
  }
]

Keep the top-level JSON schema unchanged from previous runs. Populate "recommended_reviewers" with verified reviewer usernames only, following the risk-based reviewer count. You may also include the detailed "reviewers" array for internal selection, but do not output unverified candidates.

pull_request.body:
## Analysis
Markdown file view mode reused a basic markdown renderer that did not wire the richer agent markdown components. That meant mermaid fences stayed as plain code and node links stayed as regular links even when the file preview had canvas context.

## Summary
- render Mermaid fences in markdown file preview
- render node links as chips when canvas context is available
- keep code fences on the existing code block renderer

## Tests
- cd web_src && npm test -- src/pages/app/Markdown.spec.tsx --run
- make format.js
- make check.lint.ui
- make check.build.ui

Closes #5368

data.body:
<!-- superplane-pr-risk-review -->
**Risk:** 22/100 (low)

### Summary
Enhances the MarkdownContent component to render Mermaid diagrams, node-chip links, and syntax-highlighted code blocks inside file previews, with prop-drilling of canvasId/organizationId through the files layer.

### Concerns
- rehypeRaw is used alongside rehypeSanitize; raw HTML in user-controlled markdown files could be a vector if the sanitize schema ever drifts.
- The 'node:' protocol is whitelisted in the sanitize schema; it relies entirely on MarkdownLink rendering it as a chip instead of a raw anchor.
- No test exercises the case where canvasId is provided but organizationId is absent (or vice versa) for node: links.
- MarkdownCodeWithDiagrams casts children to String unconditionally; non-string ReactNode children could produce '[object Object]' in rendered output.

**Recommended reviewers:** shiroyasha

<!-- superplane-pr-risk-review -->
**Risk:** 68/100 (high)

### Summary
Consolidates two-transaction commit-staging flow into one, but passes the outer non-transactional `db` handle instead of `tx` to `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive`, defeating atomicity and risking orphaned version rows.

### Concerns
- `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` receives `db` (non-transactional) instead of `tx`; version INSERT is not part of the surrounding transaction.
- If `publishCanvasVersionInTransaction` fails, the new version row is already committed and cannot be rolled back.
- `usageService` and `autoLayout` are accepted as parameters in `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` but never used.
- Usage quota enforcement (`usage.EnsureOrganizationWithinLimits`) present in the old path is silently dropped.
- No new tests cover the `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` function or the single-transaction path.
- Git revert is triggered inside the transaction closure on publish failure; network errors here are only logged, not propagated.

**Recommended reviewers:** forestileao

configuration.body:
<!-- superplane-pr-risk-review -->
**Risk:** 68/100 (high)

### Summary
Consolidates two-transaction commit-staging flow into one, but passes the outer non-transactional `db` handle instead of `tx` to `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive`, defeating atomicity and risking orphaned version rows.

### Concerns
- `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` receives `db` (non-transactional) instead of `tx`; version INSERT is not part of the surrounding transaction.
- If `publishCanvasVersionInTransaction` fails, the new version row is already committed and cannot be rolled back.
- `usageService` and `autoLayout` are accepted as parameters in `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` but never used.
- Usage quota enforcement (`usage.EnsureOrganizationWithinLimits`) present in the old path is silently dropped.
- No new tests cover the `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` function or the single-transaction path.
- Git revert is triggered inside the transaction closure on publish failure; network errors here are only logged, not propagated.

**Recommended reviewers:** forestileao

### Summary
Consolidates two-transaction commit-staging flow into one, but passes the outer non-transactional `db` handle instead of `tx` to `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive`, defeating atomicity and risking orphaned version rows.

### Concerns
- `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` receives `db` (non-transactional) instead of `tx`; version INSERT is not part of the surrounding transaction.
- If `publishCanvasVersionInTransaction` fails, the new version row is already committed and cannot be rolled back.
- `usageService` and `autoLayout` are accepted as parameters in `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` but never used.
- Usage quota enforcement (`usage.EnsureOrganizationWithinLimits`) present in the old path is silently dropped.
- No new tests cover the `CreateNewCanvasVersionFromLive` function or the single-transaction path.
- Git revert is triggered inside the transaction closure on publish failure; network errors here are only logged, not propagated.

**Recommended reviewers:** forestileao

values.llm_summary: Adds app-wide light/dark/system theme preference with ThemeProvider context, anti-flash script, shared CSS tokens, and dark-mode styling across ~245 frontend files, plus a DB migration that data-migrates yellow folders to slate and removes the yellow color from the CHECK constraint.
values.title: feat: add app-wide dark mode with theme preference control

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Adds per-user pin and star preferences for canvas apps via a new DB table, gRPC endpoint, and updated home page UI with a dedicated Pinned section and preference-sorted layout.
values.title: feat: star and pin apps

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Adds a derived warning to action nodes with no valid incoming connections, surfacing it in both the canvas editor and log sidebar, with supporting unit tests.
values.title: fix: warn on unconnected action nodes

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Adds enforcement at both staging (canvas_patcher) and publishing (canvas_publisher) layers that blocks update_node from swapping a node's existing implementation, while allowing a placeholder node to receive its first implementation; prompt and tool-schema documentation is updated to match.
values.title: fix: reject node implementation changes

values.author: andrecalil
values.llm_summary: Adds localStorage-based persistence of the last visited organization per account, auto-redirecting users on login while allowing explicit org switching via a new `?select=true` query parameter.
values.title: feat: Remember Last Selected Org

values.author: lucaspin
values.llm_summary: Fixes staged canvas draft changes leaking into live view by extracting canvas resolution logic into utility modules and gating the staged-read flag to edit sessions on the live version only.
values.title: fix: keep staged canvas changes out of live view

values.author: andrecalil
values.llm_summary: Adds `min-w-0` to a flex container and `wrap-anywhere` to a textarea so long secret values wrap correctly in the Add Secret dialog.
values.title: fix: Wrap Text on the Add Secret Dialog

values.author: ahfoysal
values.llm_summary: Migrates local secret AES-GCM encryption to use the immutable secret UUID as associated data instead of the mutable name, with backward-compatible fallback decryption for legacy name-bound secrets.
values.title: fix: support id-bound local secret data

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Removes horizontal scroll from the organization members table by adding overflow/whitespace Tailwind overrides and truncating long email addresses with a tooltip.
values.title: fix: remove horizontal scroll from the members table

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Adds a new Sentry integration action that links an existing GitHub issue to a Sentry issue via the Sentry GitHub integration, including backend Go implementation, frontend mapper, tests, and documentation.

values.author: skiba111
values.llm_summary: Adds five new EC2 integration actions (RunInstance, StartInstance, StopInstance, RebootInstance, GetInstanceStatus) with tests, partially overlapping with the existing ManageInstancePower action.
values.title: feat(ec2): add EC2 instance lifecycle management actions

values.author: skiba111
values.llm_summary: Preserves native Go types (float64, bool, int) when a configuration field value is a single pure expression, fixing HTTP actions that reject stringified numeric/boolean JSON fields.
values.title: fix: preserve native types for pure expressions in JSON body fields

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Replaces `%v` with `%w` in three `fmt.Errorf` calls so errors remain unwrappable via `errors.Is`/`errors.As`.
values.title: fix: preserve context cancellation error chain

values.author: lucaspin
values.llm_summary: Fixes UI race conditions after a staging commit by using flushSync to synchronously exit edit mode before cache invalidation, switching from stale-closure state to a ref for editSessionActive, reordering cache operations so hooks read the new version ID before invalidation triggers refetches, and cleaning up dead Go/TS code.
values.title: fix(ui): stabilize transitions after commit

values.author: shiroyasha

values.author: lucaspin
values.llm_summary: Improves the display label for canvas versions lacking a commit message by falling back to a formatted createdAt timestamp instead of the generic 'Commit' string.
values.title: fix: use timestamp fallback for versions without commit messages

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Removes the ServeMux pointer dependency from GatewayAuthorizationMiddleware to fix a potential nil-pointer panic on auth failures, replacing it with a standalone writeGatewayHTTPError helper, and adds tests covering both error and denial paths.
values.title: fix: prevent auth middleware panic on gateway authorization failures

values.author: krishna28004

values.author: lucaspin

values.title: chore: update eslint and go unit test coverage budgets

values.author: shiroyasha

values.title: fix: prevent canvas websocket update loop

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Guards against a crash in the approval mapper's subtitle function when `metadata.records` is absent at runtime, and adds two focused unit tests covering the missing-records and happy-path cases.
values.title: fix: prevent approval mapper crash on missing records

values.llm_summary: Replaces a TOCTOU find-then-create pattern for invite links with an atomic PostgreSQL upsert (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), and consolidates InTransaction function variants into unified db-param signatures.
values.title: fix: prevent invite-link creation race

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Guards against null/undefined metadata in the Message display component by adding a safe-cast helper and explicit type checks, with three new unit tests covering the edge cases.
values.title: fix: prevent crash when display message metadata is missing

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Adds a `beforeSend` hook to drop Sentry events whose entire stack originates from `@dash0/sdk-web`, and extends the existing console-ignore regex to also match `Failed to fetch` messages.
values.title: fix: filter Dash0 SDK fetch failures from Sentry

values.author: felixgateru
values.llm_summary: Adds two new AWS CloudWatch alarm management components (updateAlarm and deleteAlarm) with Go backend implementations, TypeScript frontend mappers, and unit tests.
values.title: feat: add AWS EC2 updateAlarm and deleteAlarm components

values.author: lucasdelafuentep
values.llm_summary: Adds a `useSidebarSettings` hook that persists two sidebar display toggles (integration status visibility and connected-integrations-on-top sort order) to localStorage, and wires them into the BuildingBlocksSidebar UI via a new settings dropdown.
values.title: fix: Persist Building Blocks sidebar display settings

values.author: andrecalil
values.llm_summary: Increases the maximum webhook/event payload size from 64 KB to 512 KB across config constants, the webhook trigger handler, the public HTTP server, tests, and documentation.
values.title: fix: Increase Max Payload Size

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Introduces a content-key tracking mechanism so the canvas viewport re-fits the whole graph when switching between canvases or versions, rather than restoring the previous viewport.
values.title: fix: fit view to whole graph when switching canvas or version

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Adds a single useEffect to AutoCompleteInput that re-positions the fixed dropdown via requestAnimationFrame whenever a window scroll or resize event fires while the dropdown is open.
values.title: fix: keep expression autocomplete anchored to input on scroll

values.author: lucaspin
values.llm_summary: Moves name and description columns from workflow_versions to workflows table, removing denormalized metadata from version rows and simplifying related queries throughout the codebase.
values.title: fix: move app name/description to workflows table

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Removes the spurious `onChange(undefined)` call when switching between Fixed and Expression tabs in IntegrationResourceFieldRenderer, preserving the user's input, and adds three targeted React Testing Library tests to confirm the behaviour.
values.title: fix: preserve field value when toggling between Fixed and Expression

values.author: re-pixel
values.llm_summary: Prevents {{ }} expression resolution in code-typed text fields (javascript, python, shell) to avoid producing syntactically broken or unsafe runner scripts.
values.title: fix: ignore superplane expressions in runner scripts

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Replaces a hardcoded description string for the $ autocomplete token with the value from the suggestion object, and updates that value to be more precise.
values.title: fix: clarify $ expression description in autocomplete

values.author: lucaspin
values.llm_summary: Removes the unused ApplyCanvasAutoLayout gRPC endpoint across proto, Go backend, auth rules, and frontend hook, along with dead save-hook options.
values.title: chore: remove unused ApplyCanvasAutoLayout endpoint and dead save hook options

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Extracts shared markdown-code utilities into a new lib file and adds a copy button, horizontal scrolling, and text-selection support to code blocks inside canvas annotation notes, backed by a new E2E test.
values.title: fix: make code blocks in notes copyable, selectable, and scrollable

values.author: re-pixel
values.llm_summary: Removes the two-layer machine-type/fleet-ID mapping so that user-facing select values and broker fleet IDs are now identical (e1-large-amd64, etc.), resolving issue #5615.
values.title: chore: align runner machine types, remove unneccessary mapping

values.author: washingtonkk
values.llm_summary: Removes the standalone `cursor.getLastMessage` action and merges conversation-fetch functionality into `LaunchAgent`, enriching its success output with `messages` and `lastMessage` fields via a best-effort fetch.
values.title: feat: return conversation messages from Cursor Launch Cloud Agent

values.author: re-pixel
values.llm_summary: Introduces language-aware code-literal formatting for template expressions in shell, Python, and JavaScript runner script fields, replacing naive string substitution to prevent syntax breakage and injection.
values.title: fix: resolve expressions in runner scripts safely

values.author: usernamenenad
values.llm_summary: Adds six new S3-compatible actions (CreateBucket, DeleteBucket, UploadObject, DeleteObject, ListObjects, PresignedURL) to the Hetzner integration by implementing a custom AWS SigV4 HTTP client rather than using the official AWS S3 SDK.

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Removes the default system boot message so the agent stays idle on canvas open/refresh, only activating on explicit template installs or user messages.
values.title: fix: stop agent from acting unsolicited on canvas open/refresh

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Single-line CLI help text fix replacing a stale 'change-request creation' reference with 'auto-publishing' for the --draft-id flag.
values.title: fix: remove stale change request CLI reference

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Replaces React Flow's built-in `onlyRenderVisibleElements` with a custom padded-viewport culling hook that adds a 320px screen-space buffer, preventing visible nodes near the edge from being incorrectly hidden.
values.title: fix: stop culling canvas nodes near the viewport edge

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Adds a 15-second polling fallback hook to reconcile stuck 'streaming' UI state when WebSocket terminal events are missed.
values.title: fix: reconcile agent chat streaming status

values.author: nickdub
values.llm_summary: Refactors four PagerDuty list methods to use a generic paginatedList helper that follows offset-based pagination, fixing data truncation for accounts with more than 100 resources.
values.title: fix: Add PagerDuty pagination for ListServices, ListUsers, ListEscalationPolicies, and ListIncidents methods

values.author: matheuskafuri
values.llm_summary: Refactors CopyButton to support a labeled 'button' variant with success/error feedback, replacing three inline clipboard handlers across token-related settings pages.
values.title: fix: Show feedback on API token copy

values.author: andrecalil
values.llm_summary: Adds Storybook stories for the Canvas Runs page and the full AppPage, introducing MSW for network mocking along with large captured-fixture JSON files and associated devDependency additions.
values.title: chore: Canvas Page at Storybook

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Adds a 1500ms idle-timeout path to `FetchLiveLogRecords` so agent tool calls return buffered log records when the live NDJSON stream stalls rather than blocking indefinitely.
values.title: fix: return agent runner logs after stream goes idle

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Fixes a bug where readLiveLogRecords would block indefinitely on open streams after reaching the record limit, by returning immediately once the limit is hit instead of calling scanner.Scan() again.
values.title: fix: stop runner log fetches at record limit

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Fixes run sidebar selection focus by scoping focus requests to a canvas mode ('live' vs 'runs'), extracting viewport transition logic, merging full run executions into the inspection canvas, and falling back to cache invalidation when websocket patches cannot find their target run.
values.title: fix: keep run sidebar selection focused

values.author: felixgateru
values.llm_summary: Adds a new claude.runCodeAgent component that provisions an Anthropic Managed Agent sandbox with a scoped GitHub token, runs an autonomous coding task against a repository or existing PR, and emits a result after poll-based monitoring.
values.title: feat: add Run Claude Code Agent component

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Adds a new `runner_logs` resource to the agent `read_runtime` tool and a `logs` CLI sub-command, both fetching bounded NDJSON execution logs from the task broker via the existing live-log session endpoint.
values.title: feat: expose runner logs to cli and agents

values.author: forestileao
values.llm_summary: Makes canvas_yaml omitted by default from agent read results, returning only metadata (canvas_yaml_bytes, canvas_yaml_omitted) unless include_canvas_yaml=true is explicitly set, and bumps the tool contract revision to v1.1.1.
values.title: fix: keep agent read results compact by default

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Adds signed RS256 OIDC JWT assertions injected as Semaphore workflow parameters so CI jobs can verify they were triggered by SuperPlane, plus a new `superplane oidc verify` CLI command to validate those tokens.
values.title: feat: OIDC execution tokens for CI workflow authorization

values.author: shiroyasha
values.llm_summary: Mechanical migration of all JWT usage from golang-jwt/jwt/v4 to v5, updating the Claims interface, timestamp types, and audience validation patterns.
values.title: chore: migrate SuperPlane JWT usage to jwt/v5

values.author: washingtonkk
values.llm_summary: Adds file attachment support (images, PDFs, text) to Claude and OpenAI text prompt components by uploading files to each provider's Files API and referencing them via file_id, replacing the old inline approach and raising per-file size limits from 100 KB to 25 MB.
values.title: feat: allow files to be attached to openai and claude text prompt components

values.author: forestileao

values.title: fix: resolve missing expression tokens as null

values.title: fix: silence Dash0 telemetry export noise in Sentry

values.title: fix: silence Dash0 telemetry export noise in Sentry

values.title: fix: keep agent tool descriptions within provider limit

values.author: lucaspin

values.title: fix: process pending requests for deleted nodes

pull_request.body:
## Analysis
Markdown file view mode reused a basic markdown renderer that did not wire the richer agent markdown components. That meant mermaid fences stayed as plain code and node links stayed as regular links even when the file preview had canvas context.

## Summary
- render Mermaid fences in markdown file preview
- render node links as chips when canvas context is available
- keep code fences on the existing code block renderer

## Tests
- cd web_src && npm test -- src/pages/app/Markdown.spec.tsx --run
- make format.js
- make check.lint.ui
- make check.build.ui

Closes #5368

## Analysis
The YAML diff modal used the default diff context styling, so unchanged rows inside a hunk could look highlighted alongside actual additions and removals.

## Summary
- make unchanged context rows neutral
- keep add/remove rows colored and inline word highlights stronger
- add a focused modal options test

## Tests
- cd web_src && npm test -- src/pages/app/CanvasYamlDiffModal.spec.tsx --run
- make format.js
- make check.lint.ui
- make check.build.ui

Closes #5366

pull_request.body:
## Analysis
Markdown file view mode reused a basic markdown renderer that did not wire the richer agent markdown components. That meant mermaid fences stayed as plain code and node links stayed as regular links even when the file preview had canvas context.

## Summary
- render Mermaid fences in markdown file preview
- render node links as chips when canvas context is available
- keep code fences on the existing code block renderer

## Tests
- cd web_src && npm test -- src/pages/app/Markdown.spec.tsx --run
- make format.js
- make check.lint.ui
- make check.build.ui

Closes #5368

pull_request.body:
## Summary
- Add light/dark/system theme preference with anti-flash handling, shared dark-mode styling tokens, and a menu toggle in the organization header
- Polish dark mode across the canvas, console, memory/files, org settings, home/catalog, and auth flows (including error and create-organization pages)
- Remove the yellow canvas folder color (with DB migration to slate) and fix follow-up review issues for legacy folder colors, node icon Tailwind classes, and agent sidebar contrast

## Test plan
- [ ] Toggle theme via organization menu (Light / Dark / System) and reload to confirm preference persists
- [ ] Verify canvas, console, memory, and files tabs in both themes, including React Flow background and Monaco editors
- [ ] Check org settings pages (General, Secrets, Members, Integrations) and home/new-app catalog in dark mode
- [ ] Create organization and error fallback pages render correctly in dark mode
- [ ] Confirm legacy yellow folders display as slate on the apps home page
- [ ] Run `make check.build.ui` and backend tests as needed

pull_request.body:
Fixes #3710

Summary:
- store app pin/star preferences in the database per user
- return preferences from the canvas list API
- show pinned apps separately and sort starred apps first

Tests:
- make format.go
- make format.js
- make pb.gen
- make db.migrate DB_NAME=superplane_test
- make db.migrate DB_NAME=superplane_dev
- make test PKG_TEST_PACKAGES=./pkg/grpc/actions/canvases
- make test PKG_TEST_PACKAGES=./pkg/grpc/actions/canvasfolders
- cd web_src && npm test -- index.spec.tsx canvasAppPreferencePresentation.spec.ts --run
- make check.lint.ui
- make check.build.ui
- make lint && make check.build.app
